caveats: COMPANION GUIDES: many entries embed MrKraken's full one-page companion guide (RSI Community Hub). He permits reuse with credit, so we show the complete, uncropped guide image — which keeps his 'Made by the Community' mark, RSI disclaimer and socials intact — and additionally credit/link MrKraken (youtube.com/@MrKraken · @RealMrKraken). The factual text in each entry is re-expressed in our own words.

Star Citizen Alpha 4.8.x is LIVE as of late June 2026 (4.8.0 launched ~mid-May 2026; 4.8.2 LIVE 17 June 2026). Alpha 4.9.0 is in testing (Evocati from 20 June 2026, then PTU) and is NOT live. LIVE content is pinned to 4.8.x; anything 4.9-specific (Siege of Orison return, Mission Instancing) is PTU/unreleased.

VOLATILITY: aUEC reward figures, contract values, reputation thresholds and earnings-per-hour rates drift between patches and are market/server-dependent. Treat all specific numbers (payouts, rep, drives) as approximate/4.8-era and verify in-game.

PATCH DRIFT: Several entries assert content is 'LIVE through 4.8.x' though the underlying sources only document 4.3.x. Onyx Facilities are permanent PU content (not time-limited) so persistence is plausible; the explicit 4.8 presence rests on the MMOPIXEL 4.8 loot guide and the star-citizen.wiki mission API (which lists Onyx missions on patch 4.8.2-LIVE), not on the original 4.3-era guides.

COMMUNITY-SOURCED: Phase/wing walkthrough detail, P#M# shorthand, route specifics (pipes/vents/'Gamma Arm'), loot names and earnings claims come from community guides (GamingDeputy, MMOPIXEL, Solar Trade Alliance FAQ) rather than official CIG patch notes. P1M1/P2M1 etc. are guide-author shorthand, NOT official in-game labels.

NAMING CORRECTIONS: 'ASD' = Associated Sciences & Development (a real weapons-manufacturer org; NOT 'Onyx Subsurface Developments', a community misreading). 'Hockrow Agency' is the correct spelling (mis-typed 'Hockwer'/'Hackrow'). Alpha 4.3.1 is codenamed 'Virtue's Descent' (NOT 'Project Hyperion' — Project Hyperion is the in-fiction Site B storyline, not the patch name). Onyx loot: the sniper is the Volt 'Zenith' rifle and the stealth armor is 'ASD Geist' (not 'S-Geist').

SOURCING HYGIENE: starcitizen.tools, RSI support/Spectrum and several guide hosts returned HTTP 403/429 to automated fetch during verification; many wiki facts were confirmed via search snippets plus independent corroboration rather than a clean re-read. No copyrighted maps/images (e.g. MrKraken's Onyx Companion maps) were reproduced.

REWARD-DELIVERY GOTCHA: Onyx mission rewards (ASD Secure Drives / Compounds) go ONLY to the player who accepted the contract, deposited in their home-location inventory — they are not shared with the group.

DISAMBIGUATION: Two distinct things are called 'underground facilities' — (1) generic UGF bunker/cave complexes (FPS clear/loot/data missions, baseline since Alpha 3.18) and (2) the story-driven Onyx research facilities (Alpha 4.3.0+). Do not conflate. 'Save Stanton' is a separate concluded cargo/combat event, not part of the Onyx loop.

legend.meaning: Standard powered/sliding door between rooms or corridors; opens on approach or via a nearby panel.
Sealed/keycarded door requiring a hack, keycard, objective progress, or power restoration to open.
Vertical transit between sub-levels; in Onyx Facilities the main elevators descend from the surface hangars through the atrium toward the lower wings.
Lootable container/item spawn (commodities, components, gear); optional pickups, e.g. consignment dispenser output sellable at kiosks.
Mission-critical interaction point that advances the contract (file pickup, drop-off, data collection point).
Computer/mainframe/datacenter terminal; used for data uploads (insert Cryptokey) or to read/extract files.
Audio device / log left in the facility; interacting reveals narrative (e.g. circumstances of abandonment) and often counts as a data objective.
Environmental/interaction puzzle gating progress (e.g. power routing, sequence interactions).
Radiation/energy hazard zone; in Onyx Engineering the power-core anomaly pulses radiation roughly every 30 seconds.
Hostile NPCs (pirates/scavengers) or creatures (Irradiated Valakkar in Onyx engineering wings) defending the facility.
Delivery/drop point for required cargo such as a box of classified files in Security Classification UGF missions, or a low-orbit station freight elevator for Onyx Phase 2 data.
Surface ship hangar that serves as the only entry into an Onyx Facility (reached via the mission quantum marker; standard large hangar — XL hangars are incompatible).
Source of named mission rewards such as the ASD Secure Drive obtained from Onyx investigation contracts (redeemed at a Wikelo Emporium).

entries.name: Onyx Facilities (ASD)
entries.location: Stanton system, spread across multiple moons of Hurston, Crusader, ArcCorp and microTech. Naming scheme SX-YZ: X = the PLANET's orbital number; Y = letter A-D for the moon's orbital position around that planet (A closest, D farthest); Z = the facility's number on that moon. Example: S1A8 is on Arial (a moon of Hurston, Stanton 1). ~100 cloned instances documented.
entries.access: No fixed map markers and no walk-in entrance. Accept an Onyx investigation contract from Arken Mallor (Hockrow Agency) via the mobiGlas Investigation tab; the contract supplies a quantum-travel marker. Entry is through one of two surface ship hangars (standard large hangar; XL hangars are incompatible), then down via elevators through sub-levels to an atrium and the lower wings.
entries.overview: Roughly 100 abandoned underground research sites built by Associated Sciences & Development (ASD), scattered across the Stanton system. Narratively there is one facility; CIG cloned it ~100 times across Stanton's moons so many players can run the content without crowding. The exterior footprint is small (two large hangars plus a central structure); the real complex is underground. Houses an investigation mission system tied to the in-fiction Regen Crisis and Dr. Logan Jorrit's unethical experiments.
objectives: Infiltrate the facility and reach the relevant wing (Research Labs or Engineering)
Locate and interact with data collection points / audio devices to retrieve research data and audio logs
Recover personnel, engineering and research files plus anomaly/security/seismic/power data depending on the active mission track
Survive environmental hazards and clear or evade hostiles to reach objective terminals
steps: Build reputation / accept the opening Onyx contract from Arken Mallor of the Hockrow Agency
Quantum-travel to the marked facility and land/dock at one of the two surface hangars
Descend via elevators through the sub-levels toward the atrium
Enter the assigned wing (Research Labs for data/audio logs; Engineering for the power-systems track)
Interact with data collection points / audio devices to extract the required files or data
Extract back to the surface hangar and complete the contract for payout and reputation
rewards: aUEC payouts per mission
ASD Secure Drives (data drives from the investigation contracts), redeemed later at a Wikelo Emporium
Lootable items recoverable inside the facility
hazards: Facility decay: highly hazardous interior environment overall
Engineering section: hazardous power systems and radiation zones requiring environmental awareness
Power core is a large dome with a central anomaly that emits a radiation pulse roughly every 30 seconds (use shielded walls/barriers for cover)
Hostile pirates / scavengers occupying the wings
Irradiated Valakkar (hostile creatures) present in the engineering wing
tips: Phase 1 must be completed in succession to unlock the repeatable facility mission tracks, which then run in any order
Treat the radiation pulse cadence (~30s) as a movement timer in the power-core area
The SX-YZ code tells you exactly which planet/moon a facility sits on before you travel

steps: Accept an Onyx contract that points to this instance, quantum-travel to Arial, dock at the hangar, then proceed as for any Onyx Facility
rewards: Onyx contract payout (aUEC), ASD Secure Drive, lootable items
hazards: Shares the generic Onyx hazards: decay, radiation zones in Engineering, the ~30s power-core radiation pulse, pirates and Irradiated Valakkar
tips: Layout is representative of all ~100 cloned facilities; learning one instance transfers to the others

entries.name: Onyx Facilities (ASD) - player reception & feedback
entries.location: Stanton system - ~100 abandoned underground ASD research sites across moons/planets (e.g. Arial). Surface lobby/landing area, multiple wings below.
entries.access: Accept an Onyx/Hockrow contract via mobiGlas (mission-giver Arken Mallor / Hockrow Agency). Fly to the assigned facility, land at the surface lobby, descend into the wings.
entries.overview: Aggregated player verdict from Spectrum and player blogs: broadly positive on design/atmosphere/story (varied gameplay - FPS combat, pipe-fixing, audio-log/data gathering, cargo, server destruction; music/atmosphere praised) but with real pain points. The biggest recurring complaints are enemy spawning ('instant infinite spawns'), performance/lag during looting and interaction, and rewards several players called 'underwhelming' relative to effort.
objectives: Investigation-style PvE: enter the facility, navigate (sparse quest markers), reach specific wings, download/retrieve data or items (voice recorders, server blades, blank data drives, power logs) from terminals/lockers, and exit - often via a different route than you entered
steps: Accept an Onyx/Hockrow contract (mission-giver Arken Mallor / Hockrow Agency; guides also cite Eckhart, Bounty Hunters Guild and Redwind as distributors)
Fly to the assigned facility, land at the surface lobby, and descend into the wings
Follow audio logs and environmental cues to the objective terminal/locker for that phase's file or item
Retrieve the data/item, then find the exit (players report the intended exit is often a separate vent/shaft, not the entrance)
rewards: Phase 1 missions reward 5 ASD Secure Drives; Phase 2 rewards 10 ASD Secure Drives (community-reported)
ASD Secure Drives are mission-exclusive items turned in at Wikelo's Emporium for armor variants / blueprints / other rewards - NOT a direct aUEC payout
Loot inside includes special gear: the Volt 'Zenith' sniper rifle and 'ASD Geist' stealth armor (community-cited); players describe overall loot as modest
hazards: 'Instant infinite' / respawning enemy spawns that players say cause FPS freezes; several report it is more efficient to run through at stable FPS than to fight
Lag/desync especially when looting and interacting with terminals
Radiation zones / unstable power cores / failing tech / 'dangerous test subjects'
Limited exit routes that force backtracking through dangerous areas
Party-loot GOTCHA: only the player who ACCEPTED the mission receives the drive rewards - group members do not auto-receive them
tips: Bring extra ammo for repeat runs (limited ammo vs infinite-respawn enemies)
Coordinate in a 2+ group for survivability
If you only want the data, sprint to the objective at stable FPS rather than clearing every enemy
Watch your orientation in the research wing - it is the most maze-like; players got lost and walked past the exit

entries.name: Onyx Facilities (ASD) - overview and how to start
entries.location: Stanton system - across multiple moons. Community guides cite early missions pointing to moons such as Calliope, Lyria, and Cellin; ~100 facilities exist system-wide.
entries.access: Accept a contract from the Hockrow Agency (Arken Mallor) under the Investigation tab in mobiGlas. The first mission does not give a precise QT marker - quantum to the nearest marker on the target moon, contact ATC, and land in one of two large hangars (XL hangars incompatible). Subsequent phases unlock progressively.
entries.overview: The Onyx Facilities are ~100 abandoned underground ASD research complexes added in Alpha 4.3.0 ('Dark Territory', LIVE 16 Aug 2025) and expanded in 4.3.1 ('Virtue's Descent', Sep 2025). They form a story-driven PvE investigation loop: investigator Arken Mallor of the Hockrow Agency hires players to enter the derelict sites and retrieve research data to build a case against ASD's experiments tied to the Regen Crisis. The chain runs across three phases through facility wings (Personnel, Engineering, Research) and finally Site B (Dr. Jorrit's lab).
objectives: Phase 1: recover Personnel, Engineering, and Research files
Phase 2: hijack/retrieve Energy Anomaly, Security, Seismic, and Power Usage data
Phase 3 (4.3.1): return to gather new data and explore Site B - Dr. Jorrit's lab - and conduct a bio-mechanical experiment
steps: Open mobiGlas, go to the Contracts/Investigation tab and accept a Hockrow Agency (Arken Mallor) Onyx contract
Quantum-travel toward the indicated moon; jump to the nearest available marker and approach the facility
Request landing via ATC and set down in one of the two standard large hangars (avoid XL hangars)
Descend through the underground landing area into the facility and begin the wing-specific objectives
Retrieve the required data/item, then extract and hand in or deliver the data as the contract specifies
rewards: Phase 1 missions: 5 ASD Secure Drives (community-reported)
Phase 2 missions: 10 ASD Secure Drives
Phase 3 (Site B): ASD Compounds - a 2SCU container whose contents depend on the experiment outcome
ASD Secure Drives are trade goods turned in at a Wikelo Emporium for further rewards
Loot reportedly includes the Volt 'Zenith' sniper rifle and ASD 'Geist' stealth armor at weapon/armor racks (community-sourced)
hazards: Failing infrastructure and unstable/hazardous power cores
Radiation zones with timed pulses (~every 30s in reactor areas; cross using cover/barriers)
Timed sections and parkour/navigation puzzles
Elite NPC defenders (community guides name 'Sterling' elite troops, plus quick-reaction-force spawns)
Points of no return / limited exits forcing backtracking
tips: Bring radiation-resistant gear/meds and a multi-tool with a repair module for Phase 2 repair sub-objectives
Rewards go to the accepting player's home inventory and are not shared with the group
Run with a team for Phase 2/3 combat and puzzle sections

entries.name: Underground Facilities (UGFs) - generic bunker/cave complexes
entries.location: Across Stanton planets and moons (e.g. underground bunkers on Lyria such as Buckets and the abandoned Teddy's Playhouse), extended into the Pyro system.
entries.access: Travel to the surface facility marker; missions are accepted from the relevant giver (Security Organizations for legal variants, Ruto for illegal variants). Some require equipment (e.g. a Cryptokey for Data Heist).
entries.overview: The baseline 'underground facilities' of Star Citizen: generic dug-in bunker complexes on Stanton (and Pyro) planets and moons that host FPS missions. Distinct from the later Onyx research facilities. UGF missions task players with eliminating occupants, searching for data pads/consignments, dropping off classified files, or hacking a mainframe depending on the variant.
objectives: Eliminate-all: kill everyone in the designated building (FPS clear)
Retrieve Consignment: find data pads; a consignment code feeds a dispenser that ejects manifest items one by one (sellable at commodity kiosks, optional)
Security Classification: drop off at least one box of classified files at a security UGF (shareable; legal via Security Orgs, illegal via Ruto)
Data Heist: support an anonymous hacker by uploading data from the facility mainframe
steps: Accept a UGF mission from a Security Organization (legal) or from Ruto (illegal)
For Data Heist: bring a Cryptokey and insert it at the terminal in the UGF datacenter
Travel to the facility, complete the variant objective (eliminate / retrieve / drop-off / upload)
Extract and turn in / sell any recovered consignment goods
rewards: aUEC mission payouts
Lootable consignment commodities (sellable at kiosks)
Reputation with the relevant faction (Security Orgs or Ruto's network)
hazards: Hostile NPC occupants (pirates / outlaws / Nine Tails-linked actors); higher danger in Pyro UGFs
CrimeStat consequences for illegal variants
tips: Consignment item collection is optional bonus loot, not required to complete
Ruto is a no-questions-asked private contractor tied to Stanton pirate gangs incl. Nine Tails - his variants are illegal

entries.name: Underground Facility (UGF) bunker contracts - eliminate / recovery
entries.location: Bunkers/UGFs on Stanton planets and moons - notably Hurston and microTech. Accessed by flying to the facility surface entrance.
entries.access: Mercenary/security contracts via mobiGlas (e.g. associated with Hurston Dynamics, microTech / MT Protection Services, Crusader Security depending on the mission).
entries.overview: The baseline generic dug-in underground facility / bunker mission line that has existed since Alpha 3.18. Players fly to a turret-defended underground facility and clear it on foot of AI occupants (outlaws or corporate security), sometimes retrieving an item. Distinct from the story-driven Onyx Facilities.
objectives: Recovery Contract: clear outlaw scavengers from a Hurston Dynamics underground facility and retrieve a safe (return to Teasa Spaceport)
Remove Illegal Occupants: clear outlaw scavengers from a turret-defended underground facility (FPS)
Extermination Job: clear MT Protection Services personnel (~7 enemies with Custodian SMGs, medium/heavy armor) from a facility
steps: Accept a bunker/UGF security or recovery contract from mobiGlas
Fly to the facility (watch for defensive turrets on approach/landing; turrets target the ship, not on-foot)
Enter and clear the AI occupants room by room
Retrieve any required item (e.g. safe) and extract to complete
rewards: Recovery Contract: 22,500 aUEC (Constantine Hurston, Hurston Dynamics); Extermination Job: 28,000 aUEC (MT Protection Services); Remove Illegal Occupants: 14,000 aUEC; Data Heist: 60,000 aUEC. (Wiki CC BY-SA values; approximate / 4.8-era, may drift between patches.)
~90,000 aUEC for a UGF run is a community-stream headline figure (not an official payout)
Lootable gear/weapons/armor from defeated NPCs; reputation with the issuing faction
hazards: AI turrets defending the entrance; multiple armed FPS enemies inside (some heavy armor)
CrimeStat risk: clearing lawful occupants (e.g. Extermination Job) can incur CrimeStat 1+

OFFICIAL 4.8 KNOWN ISSUE: 'remove occupants' missions can spawn with no occupants present, making them uncompletable, with no current workaround; also, entering a UGF without permission gives no trespassing warning, and after turning in all area missions you have ~15 minutes before guards turn hostile
Player reports of door/elevator hang-ups and AI desync are recurrent
tips: Bring FPS gear, armor and ammo; some enemies wear heavy armor
Do not loiter after completing area missions - leave inside the ~15-minute window to avoid guard aggro
Land behind cover and approach on foot rather than flying into turret arcs
Stack multiple area contracts before going in so a single clear-out satisfies several payouts
Check the 4.8 Known Issues list - some occupant-clear missions are bugged this patch

entries.name: Data Heist (UGF mission type)
entries.location: At underground facilities (UGFs) in Stanton (e.g. microTech), designated per-mission by the giver.
entries.access: Accept the Data Heist mission (legal via Security Orgs, illegal via Ruto; community guides also reference a 'Bit Zeros' contractor reputation track). Bring a Cryptokey (portable hacking chip) before deploying - one can also be looted from an operator on site.
entries.overview: A UGF mission variant where the player supports an anonymous hacker in uploading data from the mainframe at an underground facility. Requires hardware to breach the datacenter terminal. Introduced in Alpha 3.21.1 (late 2023); still part of LIVE UGF content.
objectives: Reach the UGF datacenter
Insert a Cryptokey at the datacenter terminal
Upload/extract data from the mainframe for the hacker (multiple data packs)
steps: Acquire a Cryptokey
Travel to the designated UGF and clear/navigate to the datacenter
Allow the connection, insert the Cryptokey, and begin the data upload
Manage the overheat mechanic: servers overheat mid-upload; find security codes on data screens to re-enable cooling or lose data
Survive waves of guards spawning from elevators, then extract
rewards: ~30,000 aUEC base, scaling to ~60,000 aUEC at higher tiers (community-reported; patch-dependent)
hazards: Hostile NPC occupants of the facility and waves of UEE guards spawning during upload (CrimeStat consequences for the illegal variant)
Server overheat mechanic mid-upload; time/exposure pressure while the upload runs
tips: Do not forget the Cryptokey - it is the gating item for the datacenter terminal (or loot one from a downed operator)
Watch the overheat: re-enable cooling using on-screen security codes before data is lost

entries.name: Onyx Site B / Project Hyperion (Dr. Logan Jorrit's lab) - player bugs & gotchas
entries.location: Site B - the deepest Onyx wing, centred on Dr. Jorrit's lab and a central power core. Reached after Phase 1 & 2 data is collected.
entries.access: Requires completed/collected Phase 1 & 2 prerequisites. Approach the power core via a horizontal elevator on a damaged path; expect a puzzle plus combat layer.
entries.overview: Site B (the in-fiction 'Project Hyperion' content; added in Alpha 4.3.1 'Virtue's Descent', Sep 2025) is the climax wing, gating on prior phase data and combining FPS combat with puzzle-solving around a radiation-pulsing power core. It has dedicated community 'bugs, tips and workarounds' coverage, indicating players hit notable issues here.
objectives: Use the Phase 1 & 2 data to access Site B / Jorrit's lab
Navigate a damaged path to a horizontal elevator that leads to the power core
Handle the power-core anomaly (radiation pulses every ~30s) while completing the lab/experiment objective
steps: Confirm you have completed/collected the required Phase 1 and Phase 2 prerequisites before attempting Site B
Approach the power core via the horizontal elevator on the damaged path; expect a puzzle plus combat layer
rewards: Community FAQ lists 'ASD Compounds' and a 2SCU container as the Phase 3 / Site B reward, varying by experiment outcome (community-reported; exact contents unconfirmed)
hazards: Radiation pulses every ~30 seconds at the power core

Player-reported bugs: falling through the world into radiation pits, getting stuck in walls/railings, character clipping through geometry during combat, NPC desync (enemies spawning right in front of you or ignoring you)
Respawn-after-death reportedly dropped at least one player through the world
tips: Consult community 'Bugs, Tips, and Workarounds for Project Hyperion (Site B)' coverage before a first attempt because of world-clip/stuck risks
Move deliberately near railings/edges to reduce clip-through deaths; bring a partner to revive/cover after desync spawns

entries.name: Tactical Strike Group (TSG)
entries.location: Nyx system (community guides cite QV Extraction Station / an asteroid base). Contract giver reached via mobiGlas.
entries.access: Mercenary/Contract giver Deacon Tobin of InterSec (InterSec Defense Solutions). Requires a minimum InterSec reputation (sources vary between 'Rank III' and completing 'Contractor Rank' in the Nyx system). Open mobiGlas > Contracts (Mercenary) once the rep gate is met.
entries.overview: The flagship new PvE group activity of Alpha 4.8 'Tactical Strike'. A multi-phase combined-arms operation (ship combat + FPS) in which a group is sent to a supposedly abandoned station/asteroid base to find out what happened to a recon team. Combines fighter escort, ship-on-station assault, a 'trench run' through interior tunnels, and on-foot pilot rescue. Fully replayable; each run builds reputation and earns rewards.
objectives: Phase 1: escort and defend the People's Alliance Idris-class capital ship 'Tranquility' into position against enemy (Shattered Blade) fighter waves
Phase 2: destroy the station's armored power relays (relay doors hardened against weapons)
Phase 3: 'trench run' - traverse interior tunnels destroying ~18 cooling units fast enough to avoid distortion buildup disabling your ship
Phase 4: destroy the central core powering the station's main defensive systems
Later phases: marines board and clear the interior to an override terminal; rescue/extract the captured pilot (Gabe Windell) to complete the contract
steps: Grind InterSec reputation in the Nyx system until the TSG contract unlocks (Rank III / Contractor tier)
Accept the Tactical Strike Group contract from Deacon Tobin via mobiGlas
Assemble a group with mixed loadouts (fighters for escort, ships that can hit hardened targets, plus FPS-equipped players for boarding)
Work through the escort, relay, trench-run, core, and boarding/rescue phases in order
Extract the rescued pilot to claim blueprints, gear and aUEC
rewards: Permanent crafting blueprints (from two reward pools)
Flight suits and helmets (e.g. Racing Flight Suits, Tailwind Flight Helmets)
High-quality (grade A/B) ship components
A unique bomber jacket
Rare trade commodities
Weapon blueprints (Novia crossbow variants, Omnisky series)
aUEC and InterSec reputation
hazards: Heavy fighter waves in the escort phase - bring a capable combat ship
Distortion/ambient damage inside the trench/tunnel section can disable ships if cooling units aren't destroyed quickly
FPS combat during the boarding/rescue phase
Designed for groups - difficult or impractical solo
tips: Replayable - repeat for reputation and additional blueprint rolls

Sources disagree on the exact final phase (most describe rescuing/escorting pilot Gabe Windell; one community guide lists a final 'destroy an arriving Vanduul Mauler' phase) - treat precise phase order as community-sourced until verified
Bring ballistic weapons for the hardened relays/cooling units

entries.name: Onyx Phase 1 - Personnel / Engineering / Research file retrieval (Jorrit Dossier)
entries.location: Onyx Facilities on Stanton moons. Phase 1 routes run mainly through the upper Engineering level and the Research wing (entered from the underground hangar). Community shorthand P1M1-P1M3.
entries.access: Hockrow Agency / Arken Mallor contracts in the mobiGlas Investigation tab. On the wiki these appear as 'Jorrit Dossier' missions (e.g. 'Jorrit Dossier: Access Engineering Files'). Phase 1 must be completed to unlock later phases; 'Access Engineering Files' requires 'Onyx Personnel Files' first.
entries.overview: Phase 1 is the introductory, easiest Onyx tier. It teaches the facility layout while you retrieve three categories of files. Objectives center on finding terminals, downloading file data, and recovering physical evidence such as voice recorders. The P1M1/P1M2/P1M3 codes are a guide-author convention, not in-game labels. Canonical mission data is on api.star-citizen.wiki (missions listed on patch 4.8.2-LIVE).
objectives: Download/retrieve Personnel file data from terminals (opening mission)
Retrieve Engineering file evidence by interacting with an audio device near the center of the engineering wing (occupied by pirates/Valakkar)
Retrieve Research file evidence (voice recorder) from the research labs
Exit the facility and complete the contract
steps: P1M1 Personnel Files: from the hangar reach the Engineering level, then head into the Research wing; download personnel data from terminals (the first mission gives no precise interior objective marker per community guides)
P1M2 Engineering Files: traverse Engineering through the pipes section into a warehouse and a vent system; recover a voice recorder from a locker (watch for a point-of-no-return passage)

P1M3 Research Files: drop down a shaft to the research labs ('Research Level 7 / Analytics' per community sources), pass through a security room to a hidden exit, collect the voice recorder; multiple weapon racks available
rewards: 5 ASD Secure Drives for Phase 1 completion (confirmed via Solar Trade Alliance FAQ and the star-citizen.wiki mission API), delivered to home location, redeemable at Wikelo's Emporium
Opportunistic loot at weapon/armor racks (community-cited; specific weapon/armor names are low-confidence flavor, not API-confirmed rewards)
hazards: No direct interior objective marker on the first mission - risk of getting lost
Point-of-no-return passages requiring forward commitment
Standard facility enemies and environmental clutter
tips: Phase 1 is the lowest-difficulty tier; good for learning the wing layouts before Phase 2
Grab the weapon racks while passing through Engineering/Research

entries.name: Onyx Phase 2 - Energy Anomaly / Security / Seismic / Power data retrieval
entries.location: Onyx Facilities on Stanton moons - deeper into Engineering (control room, fuel reactor zone) and multi-level Research sections (community sources cite 'Level 6 Xenotechnology' and energy laboratories), with zero-G areas. Community shorthand P2M1-P2M4.
entries.access: Unlocks after Phase 1; accept from Hockrow Agency / Arken Mallor in mobiGlas. Deliveries for these missions are turned in at a low-orbit station freight elevator.
entries.overview: Phase 2 is the harder data-running tier. It adds environmental hazards (radiation, failing infrastructure), timed sections, repair sub-tasks, and tougher elite enemy encounters. Objectives shift from simple file downloads to extracting and rewriting data onto blank drives/server blades and delivering it off-site. A structural difference is that Phase 2 missions end with a DELIVERY to a low-orbit station.
objectives: Retrieve/rewrite Energy Anomaly, Security, Seismic, and Power Usage data onto drives/server blades
Complete in-facility repair and traversal sub-tasks
Deliver the retrieved data to a low-orbit station
steps: P2M1 Energy Anomaly Data: access the condenser area, download anomaly data, complete a repair sub-task (e.g. repair a damaged pipe), deliver to the low-orbit station
P2M2 Security Data: reach the Level 6 Xenotechnology office, retrieve and transfer security data onto a blank drive (expect an elite quick-reaction force), deliver to station

P2M3 Seismic Data: drop down a shaft, cross a zero-G area into the energy labs, collect a server blade and write the seismic data to it (use the right-hand vent if the door is locked), deliver to station

P2M4 Power Usage Data: upload power data in the Engineering control room, cross the fuel-reactor zone avoiding radiation pulses, clear elite enemies across the reactor platforms and rewrite the blade at the 'Gamma Arm' (community-only detail), exit via a bulkhead and deliver to station
rewards: 10 ASD Secure Drives for Phase 2 completion (confirmed via Solar Trade Alliance FAQ), redeemable at Wikelo's Emporium
Repeatable contracts unlock after Phase 2 completion (community-cited)
hazards: Radiation pulses on a ~30-second cycle in the power-core/reactor zone - shelter behind barriers between pulses
Elite 'Sterling suit specialists' armed with P4 weapons (community guides cite ~four per platform across ~six gantries) - bullet-spongey and aggressive
Zero-G server-blade retrieval (P2M3) adds movement difficulty
Timed sections and failing infrastructure; solo players reported struggling
tips: Squad up for Phase 2 - timed sections and the gantry fight are much easier with a partner
Carry a multi-tool with a repair module and a blank/printed drive; use maintenance kiosks to print drives if you arrive without one
Treat the reactor crossing as a timing puzzle: move in the gaps between radiation pulses
Bring substantial spare ammo for the Sterling gantry fight

entries.name: Onyx Facilities data-running contracts (Hockrow Agency / ASD investigation)
entries.location: Stanton system - facilities are cloned ~100x across Stanton's moons/planets so multiple players/instances can run them without crowding.
entries.access: Mission giver Arken Mallor of the Hockrow Agency. Contracts appear under the Investigation tab in mobiGlas. (Community sources note related contracts also offered by Eckhart, the Bounty Hunters Guild, and Redwind.)
entries.overview: The story-driven investigation contract chain set in the ~100 abandoned ASD (Associated Sciences & Development) Onyx research facilities cloned across Stanton. Players are hired by the Hockrow Agency to infiltrate the underground facilities and retrieve research data, building a case file on ASD's experiments into the regen crisis. This is the core 'data-running' loop: retrieve files and anomaly/security data, fight/avoid hazards, and extract.
objectives: Phase 1: access and retrieve Personnel, Engineering, and Research files from the first wings
Phase 2: hijack/gather data about the energy anomaly and the facility's security systems (higher difficulty)
Phase 3 (added 4.3.1): reach Site B, the deepest wing housing Dr. Logan Jorrit's lab, and conduct a bio-mechanical experiment to retrieve biological samples
steps: Pick up an Onyx investigation contract from Arken Mallor (Hockrow Agency) in the mobiGlas Investigation tab
Travel to one of the Onyx Facility sites in Stanton
Work through the facility wings retrieving the required files/data for the current phase
Extract with the data; collect ASD Secure Drives / Compounds
Redeem ASD rewards at a Wikelo Emporium station
rewards: Phase 1: 5 ASD Secure Drives (per player who accepted the mission)
Phase 2: 10 ASD Secure Drives
Phase 3: ASD Compounds (a 2 SCU container; contents depend on the Site B experiment result)
ASD Secure Drives redeemable at Wikelo's Emporium
hazards: FPS combat and environmental hazards inside the underground complexes
Rewards are NOT shared - only the accepting player receives them to their home-location inventory
Site B is the deepest/most dangerous wing
tips: P1M1/P1M2/P2M1 etc. are community shorthand (Phase/Mission), not official in-game labels
'ASD' = Associated Sciences & Development (NOT 'Onyx Subsurface Developments', a community misreading)
Reward quantities and phase content are community-sourced; verify in-game as exact values can change between patches

Only the Secure-Drive turn-in connects them.

Deposit them at a Wikelo Emporium to fulfil the collection contract
steps: Earn ASD Secure Drives by completing Onyx Phase 1/2 contracts

Deposit the required ASD Secure Drives through the Emporium freight elevator
Collect the reward from the local inventory at the turn-in location

entries.overview: An ongoing collection-and-trade contract loop run by the Banu trader Wikelo (the first non-human NPC in the game). Players accept specific collection contracts, gather the exact required items, deposit them via the Emporium freight elevator, and unlock blueprints/recipes and special-edition ship/vehicle rewards (the '...Wikelo Special' ships). Separate feature from Onyx/Hockrow/ASD, though Onyx ASD reward drives are spent here. The system was reworked in 4.2.1 (categorised tabs, removed random loot).
objectives: Accept a specific Wikelo collection/trade contract
Acquire the exact required items/commodities it asks for
Deposit the items through a station's freight elevator
steps: Fly to a Wikelo Emporium station (Dasi/Selo/Kinga) and enter the bazaar
Accept a collection contract and note the exact required items
Source the items (buy/haul/mine/loot, or spend ASD Secure Drives from Onyx runs)

Collect the blueprint/ship/vehicle from that station's local inventory

Armor, weapons and ship components

Contracts require exact items in exact quantities
tips: Reworked in 4.2.1 into categorised tabs (Wikelo: Vehicles, Wikelo: Other Items) with random loot removed

entries.name: Refueling contracts (United Wayfarers Club / fuel-runner missions)
entries.location: Accessed via the mobiGlas Contracts app. Top-tier contracts cluster in the Pyro system; community guides recommend basing out of Checkmate Station (Pyro). Lower-tier contracts available system-wide.
entries.access: Issued by the United Wayfarers Club faction (introduced in Alpha 4.8.0). Reputation gated: grind lower-tier refueling contracts up to Senior Contractor (~6,800 rep, community-reported) to unlock the high-value missions. Requires a MISC Starfarer or Starfarer Gemini.
entries.overview: A dedicated refueling mission line emphasized in Alpha 4.8 ('improvements to refueling gameplay and dedicated missions'). Players with a Starfarer act as mobile fuel tankers, accepting contracts to refuel ships, fleets and satellites. Higher reputation unlocks far more lucrative urgent/bulk refuel contracts. Some contracts mix refueling with combat ('refueling ambush' missions, roughly 1 in 10). A strong current aUEC earner.
objectives: Fly to the client ship/fleet/satellite and transfer the requested fuel (usually heavy on hydrogen, some quantum fuel)
Higher tiers: 'Urgent Fleet Refuel' bulk contracts for large payouts
Ambush variants: survive/fight off attackers while completing the refuel
steps: Acquire a Starfarer (or Gemini) and start taking United Wayfarers Club refueling contracts
Grind reputation toward Senior Contractor
Base at Checkmate Station (Pyro) and reroll for high-value urgent refuel contracts
Manage fuel ratios, complete deliveries, repeat
rewards: aUEC - low/mid contracts modest; 'Urgent Fleet Refuel' (Pyro) community-reported at ~1.8M aUEC as of 4.8.x (the official mission DB shows a per-SCU formula, so the exact figure is patch/market-dependent and may drift)
Blueprints for better fuel nozzles
Senior Contractor refueling loop cited as a top 4.8 money method (community headline rates of 5M-9M aUEC/hour are illustrative, server- and reroll-dependent)
hazards: Ambush/combat refuel missions can spawn hostiles (~1 in 10)
Requires careful hydrogen vs quantum tank management on the Starfarer (six tanks)
Pyro is a lawless/dangerous system
tips: Real payout of the urgent fleet refuel is higher than the contract-list display value
Reroll contracts and run on stable servers for the best aUEC/hour

entries.name: Return of XenoThreat
entries.location: INS Jericho (UEE Navy carrier) near the MIC-L1 gas cloud, Stanton system
entries.overview: Return of XenoThreat is the server-wide combat dynamic event headlining Alpha 4.8. The terrorist faction XenoThreat pushes a fleet into Stanton, and the Civilian Defense Force (CDF) puts out an open call for pilots to help the UEE Navy repel them at the carrier INS Jericho near the MIC-L1 gas cloud. It plays out in three escalating phases — a scout-clearing sweep, a salvage-and-resupply run, and a mass capital-ship battle against incoming Hammerheads and an Idris-M frigate. Participation is tracked as CDF reputation in the mobiGlas Delphi app, and players who reach the qualifying reputation tier earn account-bound CDF cosmetics plus a Spectrum badge after the event window closes.
objectives: Accept the CDF contract from Rowena Dulli via the mobiGlas Contract/Mission Manager to register your participation
Phase 1 (Scout): fly to the designated event coordinates and destroy the XenoThreat vanguard/scout forces
Phase 2 (Resupply): recover cargo crates of Diluthermex, AcryliPlex Composite and Zeta-Prolanide from destroyed Starfarer supply wrecks in the debris field and tractor-beam them to INS Jericho
Phase 3 (Final battle): defend INS Jericho against the main XenoThreat fleet — multiple Hammerheads escorting an Idris-M frigate — until the fleet is destroyed
Stay through the cargo and combat phases so contribution/reputation points register
steps: Wait for the event window to be active, then open your mobiGlas and accept the Civilian Defense Force contract offered by Special Agent Rowena Dulli in the Contract Manager — this is mandatory, as rewards and reputation only accrue once you have accepted it.
Phase 1 - Scout: quantum to the marked coordinates near INS Jericho / MIC-L1 and clear the incoming XenoThreat vanguard and scout craft. Light and medium fighters are ideal here.

Phase 2 - Resupply: in the debris field, locate the wrecked Starfarer supply tankers and use a tractor beam (handheld multi-tool or ship/vehicle tractor) to pull free crates of Diluthermex, AcryliPlex Composite and Zeta-Prolanide. Haul each crate back to INS Jericho's hangar/deck to restock the carrier.

Phase 3 - Final battle: once Jericho is resupplied, the main XenoThreat fleet jumps in. Engage the escorting fighters and Hammerheads, and concentrate torpedo-capable ships on the Idris-M frigate until the fleet is wiped out.

After the event concludes, check the Delphi reputation app for your CDF standing — reaching the qualifying tier grants the cosmetic rewards, and the Spectrum badge is issued in the week after the event ends.
rewards: aUEC payouts for completing event contracts and contributing across the phases (community guides report up to several hundred thousand aUEC for high contribution; exact totals are not officially published)
CDF reputation progress tracked in the mobiGlas Delphi app

At the qualifying reputation tier (community-reported as 'Veteran First Responder Standing' — you do not need to fully fill the bar): account-bound CDF-themed cosmetics — an exclusive helmet, flight suit and chest armor
An exclusive (updated) CDF Spectrum badge for all qualifying participants, distributed in the week following the event
hazards: Server-wide PvPvE event — other players (including griefers) can be present and hostile around the staging area
Capital-ship-scale combat in the final phase: Hammerhead turret fire and the Idris-M's heavy weapons can destroy fighters quickly
Reputation/rewards only count if you accepted Rowena Dulli's CDF contract first — joining the fight without it earns you nothing
Leaving early or dying repeatedly during the cargo and combat phases can cost contribution points needed for rewards
Tractor-beam cargo handling in a contested debris field is slow and leaves you exposed to enemy fighters
tips: Accept the CDF contract before doing anything else so all your kills and deliveries are credited.
Bring a tractor beam (a handheld multi-tool with attachment, or a ship/vehicle with tractor capability) for the resupply phase — it is required to move the Starfarer crates.
Match your ship to the phase: nimble fighters (e.g. Gladius-class) for scouts and escorts; torpedo bombers (e.g. Eclipse, Retaliator with Size 9 torpedoes) saved for the Idris-M in the final battle.
Group up or join an org/event squad — capital ships go down far faster with coordinated fire and the event is built around mass participation.
Stay for the full cargo and combat phases; contribution is checked across both, and you only need to hit the qualifying reputation tier (not max it) to earn the cosmetics.
entries.access: Joinable in-game during the active event window: accept the Civilian Defense Force contract from Rowena Dulli in the mobiGlas Contract/Mission Manager, then travel to the event marker at INS Jericho near MIC-L1. Free to all backers on the live Alpha 4.8.x servers.

entries.name: Siege of Orison
entries.patch: Legacy version recurs on Live (current Alpha 4.8.x); a reworked, instanced version is in PTU for Alpha 4.9 (reached Evocati around 20 Jun 2026).
entries.location: Crusader, Orison (Inspiration Park district cluster) — staging at the Cloudview Center.
entries.overview: A recurring, dynamic ground-combat event in which players join the Civilian Defense Force to help Crusader Security retake Orison's Inspiration Park platforms from an occupying Nine Tails pirate force led by commander Mendo Ren. Played mostly on foot, you liberate three captured platforms, kill the Nine Tails lieutenant guarding each (each holds an IFFI access key), then travel to the Admin Center to defeat Mendo Ren and recover the final key, restoring Crusader's control of the airspace. The legacy Live version is documented on the Star Citizen Wiki. Alpha 4.9 reworks it into an instanced, party-private co-op FPS mission (no ship combat) with a new Arrival Center staging flow — those specifics are PTU/Evocati and subject to change. NOTE: the legacy Siege of Orison recurs on the 4.8.2 LIVE server (check if currently active); a reworked instanced version is in development on PTU for Alpha 4.9.
objectives: Accept the priority contract "End Nine Tails' Occupation at Orison" (only available while the event is active)
Liberate Solanki Platform and kill lieutenant Remy Kettle to take his IFFI access key
Liberate Brushwood Platform and kill lieutenant Devin Marcus to take his IFFI access key
Liberate Hartmoore Platform — destroy the Nine Tails anti-air turrets, then kill lieutenant Nolan Acker to take his IFFI access key
Travel to the Admin Center, clear its air defenses, and kill commander Mendo Ren to recover the final IFFI key and disable the no-fly zone
steps: Before starting, gear up: there are no weapon/armor shops at Orison, so buy full armor sets and weapons elsewhere (e.g. Seraphim Station) and bring them. A heavy-armor loadout, an assault rifle (e.g. P4-AR), spare ammo and medical items (e.g. a stim) are recommended.
Travel to Orison and set your respawn/regen point at the General Hospital in the Cloudview Center so deaths don't send you far away.
Open mobiGlas (F2) -> Contract Manager -> Generals/Priority and accept "End Nine Tails' Occupation at Orison". Optionally also take the "A Call to Arms" bounty for per-kill pay.

Go to the Crusader Industries Showroom at the Cloudview Center and ride the elevator to the rooftop, where Crusader Security has set up a medical forward operating base and a shuttle service to the occupied platforms.
Phase 1: Take the shuttle to Solanki Platform. Clear the Nine Tails, defeat lieutenant Remy Kettle, and loot his IFFI access key.
Phase 2: Move to Brushwood Platform. Defeat lieutenant Devin Marcus and his troops and take the second IFFI key (loot enemies/lockers along the way).
Phase 3: Move to Hartmoore Platform (a Crusader Industries military showroom). Knock out the Nine Tails anti-air turrets first, then defeat lieutenant Nolan Acker for the third IFFI key.

Phases 4-5: With three IFFI keys, head to the Admin Center. You can only reach it by ship, so deal with the anti-air turrets and defense systems, land, then fight through to commander Mendo Ren (around the central cargo barge). Defeating him yields the final IFFI key and lifts the no-fly zone, completing the event.

Alpha 4.9 rework (PTU): instead of a direct shuttle, take a tram to a new Arrival Center (a copy of the Orison shuttle hub) that acts as a shared staging area; take an elevator there to enter a private instance for you and your party. Per-island objectives become: clear the Nine Tails, reset the Security Network Control Towers, defeat the island Lieutenant, and flip the IFFI to restore airspace control, then move to the next of three islands, ending with the final boss. Ship combat is removed.
rewards: aUEC mission payout for completing the event (legacy; exact figure not reliably sourced — omitted)
Optional per-kill aUEC bonus if the "A Call to Arms" bounty is run alongside it
Lootable gear from defeated Nine Tails: weapons, armor, ammo, medical and utility items
Special keys dropped by lieutenants/enemies that unlock loot lockers and containers
hazards: No weapon or armor vendors at Orison — arrive fully equipped or you'll have to leave and resupply
Large numbers of Nine Tails NPCs and lieutenant bosses; dying without a local regen point means a long trip back
Anti-air turrets and air defenses guard Hartmoore and especially the Admin Center — ship approaches are dangerous
The Admin Center is ship-access only in the legacy version, so you need a flyable ship staged
Legacy event is known to glitch/bug out, sometimes blocking progress; it only appears while the dynamic event is active
4.9 instanced version is PTU/Evocati and has known issues; flow, party rules and objectives may change before release
tips: Group up: it's a co-op event and lieutenants/platforms are far easier with teammates. In 4.9, instances are capped at a 4-player party and locked to your group on entry, so invite before you go in.
Bring multiple full loadouts or a backup ship of gear — resupply at Orison isn't possible.
Set your medical regen at the Orison hospital before you start to minimize downtime after deaths.
Loot lieutenants for their keys to open locked containers/lockers for extra gear.
Run the "A Call to Arms" bounty simultaneously to get paid per kill on top of the event reward.
Save the Admin Center for last — you need all three platform IFFI keys before Mendo Ren's fight is worthwhile.

entries.name: Jumptown (2.x / Return to Jumptown)
entries.patch: Originally Alpha 3.16; expanded as Jumptown 2.0 (3.16) and 2.1 (May 2023). Recurs as a periodic dynamic event in the live Stanton system; verify against the current Alpha 4.8.x build.
entries.location: Stanton system. The active drug-production facility rotates between several hidden sites that do not appear on the starmap. entries.overview: A recurring, drug-running dynamic event built around an automated contraband-production lab hidden somewhere in Stanton. When live, smugglers rotate which of several known factories is active, so the lab's location changes between occurrences. Inside the facility a dispenser steadily pumps out boxes of finished drugs; players race to grab as many boxes as possible and haul them to a sell/turn-in point for a large payout. Because the loot pile is contested, Jumptown is one of the game's most notorious open-PvP flashpoints: rival players and orgs frequently camp the lab and the routes out of it. The event offers two parallel mission paths depending on your criminal standing, each ending at a different destination but sharing the same grab-the-boxes core loop.
objectives: Open the Mission Manager and accept the active Jumptown priority mission to reveal the current lab coordinates and your matching drop-off point
Travel to the hidden lab (no quantum-travel marker on the lab itself; approach from a nearby orbital marker)
Secure the interior and reach the drug dispenser
Collect contraband boxes as they spawn and load them onto your ship
Deliver/sell the boxes at the path-appropriate destination before being killed or intercepted
steps: Open mobiGlas and go to the Mission Manager, Priority category. Lawful players pick the mission under the General tab; unlawful players pick it under the Personal tab. The accepted mission shows both the lab's coordinates and where to turn the boxes in.

Check your CrimeStat first: the lawful (General) path requires CrimeStat 0, while the unlawful (Personal) path requires CrimeStat 1 or higher. You can only see/accept the path that matches your standing.

Fly to the active lab. The facility has no quantum-travel ping of its own, so quantum to the nearest orbital marker on the host moon (Yela / Lyria / Calliope) and close the remaining distance under normal/hydrogen flight.
Enter the lab on foot and clear or avoid any other players or hostiles already inside. The 2.1 layout has a larger interior, a second entrance, windows/skylights, and two dispensers.

Find the drug dispenser. It releases roughly one box of contraband at a time (community reports cite about a 30-second cadence, with the next box's timer starting only after you take the current one).
Use a Multi-Tool fitted with a tractor-beam attachment to grab each small box, then carry/tractor it onto your ship. Bring cargo space (a small dropship or cargo hauler is common).

When you have a worthwhile load, leave fast. LAWFUL: haul the boxes to the nearest law-enforcement / security port tied to that lab's location and turn them in for the drug value plus an aUEC bonus. UNLAWFUL: haul them to GrimHEX and sell them there for the box value plus a bonus.
Repeat runs if the lab stays contested-but-survivable, or extract early if the server is crowded with campers.
rewards: Large aUEC payout: the sale value of the collected drug boxes plus a completion/bonus uplift per delivered box
Lawful path: turn boxes in at the assigned LEO/security port for the drug value plus an aUEC bonus, with no need to fence the goods
Unlawful path: sell boxes at GrimHEX for the per-box price plus a bonus (the Personal-tab mission can carry a buy-in)
One of the highest aUEC-per-hour activities when runs succeed, which is exactly why it draws heavy PvP
hazards: Open PvP hotspot: players and orgs routinely camp the lab interior, the doorways, and the exit routes to steal your boxes or kill you on the way out
Air support: hostile crews often post ships overhead to gun down anyone leaving the facility on foot or in a small craft
Loot is fully lootable on death; getting killed means your gathered boxes can be taken by whoever downed you
Slow dispenser cadence forces you to linger, increasing exposure time the longer you stay to maximize a haul
Lawful runners with CrimeStat 0 can be attacked too, and may incur a CrimeStat if they shoot first in defense
Hard-to-reach lab (no QT marker) means a long, vulnerable approach and exit
tips: Confirm the active site from the Mission Manager every time - the lab rotates between Jumptown (Yela), Paradise Cove (Lyria), and Raven's Roost (Calliope), so last week's coordinates may be wrong.
Match your path to your CrimeStat: CrimeStat 0 -> General/lawful (deliver to LEO port); CrimeStat 1+ -> Personal/unlawful (sell at GrimHEX).
Bring a Multi-Tool with a tractor-beam attachment - the boxes are gathered by tractoring, not by hand-carrying alone.
Go in with a group or org for security; solo runners are easy prey for campers and overhead gunships.
Don't get greedy - grabbing a modest stack (e.g. a handful of boxes) and extracting early dramatically improves survival on a busy server versus camping for a full hold.
Quantum to the nearest orbital marker and approach quietly; the lab has no QT ping, so a careful approach can avoid tipping off campers.
entries.access: Free-to-participate in-game dynamic event; no real-money or special unlock required. Pick the path that matches your CrimeStat: lawful via the Mission Manager General tab (CrimeStat 0), unlawful via the Personal tab (CrimeStat 1+).

entries.name: Pyro Contested Zones
entries.patch: Introduced in Alpha 4.0; live presence in Alpha 4.8.x is community-reported
entries.location: Pyro system — interiors of the abandoned space stations Checkmate, Ruin, and Orbituary; support sites at the PYAM-SUPVISR asteroid bases (Pyro III L4/L5 vicinity) and the PYAM-EXHANG Executive Hangar asteroid bases south-west of the Pyro star.
entries.overview: Pyro Contested Zones are recurring free-fire PvPvE objective areas built into the interiors of three derelict Pyro space stations — Checkmate, Ruin, and Orbituary. Players raid the station interiors on foot, fighting both NPC defenders and rival players, to recover keycards, fuses, and seven unique "compboards." The compboards are the gateway item: collecting all seven and inserting them at an Executive Hangar asteroid base unlocks a vault containing a free claimable ship, rare FPS weapons, and Grade-A ship components. Rather than a timed seasonal event, this functions as a persistent, repeatable endgame loop that runs continuously in the live build. Wiki-confirmed mechanics; its ongoing presence in Alpha 4.8.x is community-reported.
objectives: Reach one of the three Pyro Contested Zone stations: Checkmate, Ruin, or Orbituary
Fight through NPCs and rival players to navigate the station interior
Collect the locked-away keycards and fuses needed to open progressively deeper doors
Recover all seven unique compboards spread across the three stations
Travel to an Executive Hangar asteroid base and insert all seven compboards
Claim the ship and loot the hangar before extracting
steps: Get to a station. Fly to one of the three primary Contested Zones in Pyro — Checkmate, Ruin, or Orbituary — and dock/land to enter the interior on foot.

Understand the door types. Interiors are gated by four lock types: Fuse Doors (insert a fuse; door opens for about 30 seconds then auto-closes), Keycard Doors (insert the matching colored keycard into the slot below the panel), Supervisor Doors (only two exist — one in Checkmate, one in Orbituary — opened with a red Supervisor Keycard), and Timer Doors (security doors that cycle open/closed on a schedule).

Print a red Supervisor Keycard if needed. Red Supervisor Keycards are fabricated at the PYAM-SUPVISR asteroid bases near Pyro III L4/L5 (e.g. PYAM-SUPVISR-3-4 and 3-5); the printer has a cooldown (community-reported ~30 minutes) before it can print again.

Work each station for its boards. The seven compboards are distributed across the three stations (community guides report roughly 3 at Checkmate and 2 each at Ruin and Orbituary), each sitting behind fuse, keycard, supervisor, or timed doors — so collect fuses/keycards as you push deeper.
Survive the contest. Throughout, expect armed NPC defenders and other players raiding the same zone; the area is free-fire, so the boards can be taken off your body if you die.

Bring all seven compboards to an Executive Hangar. Travel to a PYAM-EXHANG Executive Hangar asteroid base (south-west of the Pyro star) and insert the seven compboards into their receptacles in the zero-g area.

Wait for the power/light cycle and enter. The hangar opens on a timed cycle (community-reported: insert when the indicator lights go green, then a limited window to enter); once triggered, the door to the hangar/Engineering stays open for about 30 minutes.

Claim and loot. Inside, claim one of the available reward ships (one per player, kept until the next wipe), then sweep the hangar for rare FPS weapons and Grade-A components before extracting via the freight elevator.
rewards: A free claimable ship — one of seven available variants reported across community guides, including the Drake Cutlass Black, Drake Corsair, Anvil F7A Hornet Mk II, and Anvil F8C Lightning (each delivered with pre-installed Grade-A components and, for some, a unique skin)
Rare FPS weapons (e.g. grenade launchers and railguns) found inside the Executive Hangar
Grade-A ship components recoverable from the station interiors and the hangar
Reputation gains and high-value salvageable loot/currency from the raid
hazards: Free-fire PvPvE: hostile NPC defenders plus rival players competing for the same loot — you can be killed and looted
Carried compboards and keycards can be lost on death, so a wipe can cost an entire run
Auto-closing Fuse Doors give only about a 30-second window — being caught on the wrong side can trap or expose you
Timer Doors and the hangar power cycle force you to plan around fixed schedules, often under fire
Keycard printers and timed mechanics have had bugs (keycard printer timers are listed as a known issue in 4.8.x), so cycles may not behave reliably
Hostile, hazardous station environments typical of lawless Pyro — manage oxygen, armor, and ammo for a long interior fight
tips: Print your Supervisor Keycard first at PYAM-SUPVISR-3-4/3-5 before committing to a station, since the printer has a cooldown and only two Supervisor Doors exist
Carry spare fuses — Fuse Doors auto-close in ~30 seconds, so you often need to repeat the cycle
Run with a team: one player can hold a door or guard boards while others push deeper against NPCs and rival raiders
Use community trackers (CZ/Executive Hangar timer sites) to time the hangar power cycle and avoid wasting a board run on a closed window
Don't hoard all seven boards on one body in PvP — losing that character forfeits the whole set
Plan your extraction route (freight elevator) before you claim, so you can leave quickly once you have the ship and loot
entries.access: Open-access live PU activity — no mission acceptance required. Travel to Pyro and enter Checkmate, Ruin, or Orbituary on foot. Repeatable as often as you like; the only gating is the in-game door/keycard/compboard progression and the timed hangar cycle.

entries.name: First Contact: Alien Week
entries.location: Pledge Store (RSI website) plus the live persistent universe in Alpha 4.8.2; no single in-game location — it is a galaxy-wide seasonal celebration centered on alien manufacturers.
entries.overview: Alien Week (themed "First Contact") is one of CIG's recurring annual celebrations, running each June to mark First Contact Day — the in-universe anniversary of humanity's first peaceful encounter with the Banu (explorer Vernon Tar meeting a Banu vessel in the Davien System on 12 June 2438, leading to the Interstellar Peace and Trade Accord later that year). It is a promotional / cosmetic and ship-sale event rather than a combat objective: for the duration, the Pledge Store puts xenotech ship manufacturers (Banu, Aopoa/Xi'an, Esperia/Tevarin-Vanduul replicas, and Gatac) on sale, alongside themed paints, armor and flair, plus subscriber promotions and lore content. For 2026 it runs June 17-25 and coincides with the Alpha 4.8.2 update, which brings the Gatac Railen (a 640 SCU Xi'an-design freighter) to flyable status. There is no dedicated free-fly tied to Alien Week — broad free-fly periods in the SC calendar belong to Invictus Launch Week (May) and IAE (Nov-Dec), not this event.
objectives: Celebrate First Contact Day (humanity's first peaceful contact with the Banu) — a cosmetic / sale event, not a combat or mission objective
Browse and acquire ships from alien manufacturers while they are temporarily on sale in the Pledge Store
Sample the alien-themed cosmetics, paints, armor and lore content released for the week
steps: Read the official Alien Week comm-link on the RSI website for the year's exact start/end dates and the live ship list (2026: June 17-25).
Log into your RSI account and open the Pledge Store; the featured alien-manufacturer ships, paints and flair are listed for the limited window.
Optionally use Cross-Chassis Upgrades (CCUs): alien ships such as the Aopoa Nox (~$45 with LTI) and the Gatac Railen (~$225) are popular as CCU bridge/token ships during the sale.
Update your game client to Alpha 4.8.2 and log into the live PU to fly newly released/flight-ready alien ships such as the Gatac Railen (640 SCU).
If subscribed, check the month's Subscriber Promotions comm-link for any Alien Week subscriber flair or items.
rewards: Limited-time discounts on alien-manufacturer ships in the Pledge Store (Banu, Aopoa/Xi'an, Esperia, Gatac)
Newly flight-ready ship for the 2026 event: Gatac Railen (Xi'an-design freighter, ~640 SCU cargo) available in Alpha 4.8.2
Alien-themed cosmetics: limited ship paints/liveries, character armor and flair items
Subscriber-only promotional offers during the event month
Note: rewards are store/cosmetic in nature — Alien Week does not grant a combat-mission completion reward
hazards: This is a store-sale / cosmetic event — do not expect a free-fly. Free trial fleets belong to Invictus (May) and IAE (Nov-Dec), not Alien Week.
Pledge Store purchases use real money; ship sale pricing and CCU values are real-cash decisions, not in-game aUEC.
Beware third-party 'referral code / free 50,000 UEC' claims circulating around the event — these are referral-recruitment promotions, not an official Alien Week reward.
Sale availability is time-limited (2026: ends June 25); ships and cosmetics typically return to vault/store-unavailable afterward.
Patch tie-in (Alpha 4.8.2) means flying new ships depends on live server stability typical of a fresh point release.
tips: Alien Week recurs every June, roughly two-to-three weeks after Invictus Launch Week ends — useful for planning purchases around the annual calendar.
If you build CCU chains, the cheap Aopoa Nox is a common LTI token and the Railen (~$225) works as a mid-tier bridge toward larger targets like the Banu Merchantman or Carrack.
Check the in-game ship list before buying purely for flying — some alien ships go on sale as concepts/pledges and are not yet flyable, whereas the Railen is flight-ready in 4.8.2.
Watch for an Inside Star Citizen / This Week in Star Citizen episode and lore transmissions themed around alien cultures during the week.
Confirm the current year's exact dates and ship list from the official RSI comm-link, since the lineup changes each year.

entries.name: Operation Rock Breaker (QV Breaker Station)
entries.patch: Alpha 4.8.x (introduced in Alpha 4.7 "Welcome to the Rock")
entries.location: Nyx system — Keeger Belt asteroid field, at one of the derelict QV Breaker stations. Stage from Levski or a nearby station; the assigned breaker is roughly 50 km out from the quantum marker.
entries.overview: A multi-stage co-op/PvP mining operation set among the QV Breaker stations — colossal facilities built into asteroids in the Nyx Keeger Belt by QV Planet Services to crack open giant rocks for the minerals inside. After roughly 300 years dormant, Shubin Interstellar now hires crews to reboot one of these derelict stations: restore power, reset its systems, realign and reload a massive cutting laser, then fire it deep into the asteroid and fly inside the fresh fracture to extract the valuable ore at the core. It blends FPS combat, environmental puzzles, fabrication/loading mini-tasks, and ship mining into one long chain. Despite the "Operation" name and its launch as the flagship 4.7 feature, it is a repeatable, persistent mission type rather than a time-limited holiday event, and it remains available in the current live build — hence status "live" rather than "seasonal." Note: the rare ore is spelled "Satarax" in most guides and "Sadaryx" in others; treat them as the same material.
objectives: Reach Contractor reputation with Shubin Interstellar to unlock the contract
Accept a QV Breaker Station mining-rights contract (exclusive or shared) from the Contract Manager ship-mining section in Nyx
Restore station power by collecting and installing capacitors
Mine the required Satarax/Sadaryx ore and reset the facility systems
Recover the three randomized refractor alignment codes
Load the four beam catalysts and fabricate the three laser lenses
Align all three refractors, confirm all systems green, and fire the laser
Fly mining ships into the fracture and extract the exposed core ore
steps: Unlock access: build reputation with Shubin Interstellar up to Contractor rank (the tier above Jr. Contractor). The common fast path is to do the required intro hand-mining mission, then grind cheap repeatable iron/commodity delivery and ship-mining contracts (players report ~90 minutes / on the order of several million aUEC of throughput).

Pick up the contract: head to Nyx and take a 'QV Breaker Station mining rights' contract from the Contract Manager / ship-mining tab. Choose Exclusive for a private PvE run with secured interiors (higher entry cost, e.g. ~850k aUEC reported), or Shared for a cheaper but contested PvPvE instance with up to ~5 competing crews and higher-grade materials (~175k aUEC reported). Costs are community-reported and patch-dependent.

Phase 1 — Power & materials: enter the station (side entrances are often easier than the main door), clear hostile guards/creatures, and recover the power capacitors (commonly cited as four) from derelict mining robots scattered through the sectors. Install them at the central power core to bring the station online.

Phase 1 (cont.) — Mine ore: collect the rare ore (Satarax/Sadaryx) from greyish deposits with reddish/pink-gold veins, using the mining tool to clear ice and the salvage/repair tool to fix broken panels for access. You need at least 12 units to proceed (each lens later costs 4, so gather extra).

Phase 2 — System reset: take the tram/gondola to Operations, hit the reset console to start the facility restart, and note the three randomized refractor codes (A, B, C) — they are hidden in different parts of the station (Power Core area, Operations near the reset, and the Storage section). Record all three.

Phase 3 — Catalysts: travel through the Laser section (expect heavy FPS resistance) to the Storage area, release the four beam/lasing catalysts from a control terminal, tractor-beam them onto the trolley, and load all four into the side ports of the laser housing.

Phase 4 — Lenses & alignment: at the laser fabrication bay, craft three lenses (each consumes 4 Satarax/Sadaryx) and slot them into the laser array. Then ride the external tram to refractors A, B, and C and enter the codes you logged into the terminals at each, confirming green status.

Phase 5 — Fire & extract: at the laser control room, verify everything reads green/100% (3 refractors aligned, 3 lenses installed, 4 catalysts loaded), then hit the ignition button. Once the laser stops firing, fly your mining ship(s) into the new fracture hole and mine the exposed, valuable core ore to complete the haul.
rewards: High-value rare ore extracted from the cracked asteroid core (the main payout, sold/refined for profit)
Satarax/Sadaryx and other recovered station resources
Shubin Interstellar mining reputation progression

Exclusive runs yield more secure but lower-grade materials; shared/contested runs offer higher-quality materials at higher risk (specific aUEC payouts vary by patch and are not consistently documented)
hazards: Hostile NPC guards and creatures throughout the station; the Laser section in particular is reported as a heavy FPS fight
Shared/contested contracts are PvPvE — other crews (up to ~5) can attack you anywhere on site
Cold/environmental exposure inside the station — bring cold-resistant heavy armor (e.g. Pembroke/Morozov-class sets reported)
The core ore is unstable and harder to mine; bring well-equipped mining ships, skilled crew, and mining gadgets
Long mission with no quick exit — deaths cost a lot of progress; set up a medical/respawn capability and stock med supplies
tips: Bring a full FPS loadout (rifle/SMG plus ample ammo) plus a multitool with mining, salvage, and tractor-beam attachments; a full-size MaxLift tractor gun helps move catalysts.
Carry spare fuses/repair consumables for restoring power to doors and panels, plus med pens and a med gun.
Gather more than the minimum 12 Satarax/Sadaryx — you spend 12 just on the three lenses, so a buffer avoids a return trip.
Bring a ship with onboard respawn/medical (e.g. an Apollo/medical Ursa) so a death doesn't end the run.
Run it as a crew with split roles (combat, mining, hauling) — the chain is long and parallelizing tasks is much faster.
Carefully log all three refractor codes the first time you see them; they are randomized per run and easy to miss (the Refractor C code is often noted during the Operations reset).

Contract accepted from the Contract Manager ship-mining section in Nyx; choose Exclusive (private PvE) or Shared (contested PvPvE) instance.

entries.name: Storm Breaker
entries.patch: Introduced in Alpha 4.2.0 (LIVE 19 June 2025); persistent sandbox activity carried forward into later patches (current build Alpha 4.8.x as of mid-2026).
entries.location: Pyro system — ASD data centers on Pyro IV and the irradiated Lazarus research complexes on Pyro I.
entries.overview: Storm Breaker is a multi-stage, group-oriented sandbox operation set in the lawless Pyro system, tied to the ASD corporation and the ongoing "regen crisis" storyline. It is a full-spectrum activity: players infiltrate ASD facilities, fight escalating waves of guards in FPS combat, survive deadly radiation and mega-storms, operate vehicles and protective gear as a team, and ultimately summon and kill a giant mutated boss creature, the irradiated Apex (adult) Valakkar. Because it takes place in open Pyro, the whole chain runs in a fully open-PvP environment, so rival players are an additional threat throughout. It is designed as a persistent/recurring activity rather than a one-off mission, and it is best tackled with a coordinated team.
objectives: Collect the access/key cards needed to print an authorization card at an ASD data center on Pyro IV
Survive the multi-minute card-printing process while defending against escalating enemy waves
Travel to the irradiated Lazarus complexes on Pyro I and restore facility power
Recover a Valakkar egg/specimen from the lab and carry it to the calling tower
Summon and defeat the Apex (adult) irradiated Valakkar
Extract the loot off-site before being killed or robbed
steps: Prep before you launch: bring (or plan to find) a radiation-resistant suit such as the Stirling exploration suit, FPS weapons and armor, healing items, and ideally a crew. Expect both AI resistance and hostile players since this all happens in open-PvP Pyro.

Phase 1 - Infiltrate a Pyro IV data center: Head to one of the ASD data centers on Pyro IV. They are defended by automated turrets and staffed by scientist/guard NPCs. Acquire the various key cards needed, either by non-lethally taking down NPCs or by searching locked rooms.

Phase 2 - Print the card: Take the cards to the facility's data-processing room and start the card print. The print runs for roughly three minutes, during which you must hold the area against successive waves of attackers that escalate from lightly armored troops up to heavily armored 'citadel'-grade units.

Phase 3 - Cross to Pyro I (Lazarus): Use the transit shuttle drops to reach the Lazarus research complexes on Pyro I. These sit in extreme-radiation storm zones, so the protective suit is mandatory to survive the trip and the interior.

Phase 4 - Power up and gear up: Inside the Lazarus facility, find the ATLS IKTI rad-hardened exosuit/vehicle in the garage. Restoring its power requires an ASD maintenance key card and usually teamwork to operate. Then retrieve the irradiated Valakkar egg from the specimen cache.
Phase 5 - Summon the boss: Carry the egg to the calling/summoning tower and use it to draw out the Apex (adult) Valakkar.

Phase 6 - Kill the Valakkar: Fight the boss, dodging its irradiated bile-spit projectiles and its ground-slam attack. Focus fire its glowing green pustules, which are the weak points that take real damage.
Phase 7 - Extract: After the kill, grab the harvested rewards and haul them out via the freight elevators, which return your loot to the transit hub for extraction. Get clear before rivals catch you.
rewards: Apex Valakkar harvest: reported to drop on the order of several fangs and over a dozen irradiated pearls per kill (community figures of roughly 6 fangs and ~15 pearls)
Irradiated Valakkar eggs and irradiated pearls as high-value sellable/components
Vault Laser Prism shotgun (loot weapon associated with the activity)
Rare data drives and cosmetic gear from facility loot rooms
Access to/use of the ATLS IKTI radiation-hardened exosuit during the run
hazards: Open PvP: the entire chain runs in Pyro, so hostile players can ambush you at any phase, especially during the card print and the boss fight
Radiation: Pyro I's Lazarus zones carry lethal radiation; without a rad-resistant suit (e.g. Stirling) you take rapid damage
Mega-storms and dynamic weather: rain, cloud and storm systems cut visibility and add survival pressure
Escalating enemy waves during the ~3-minute card print, ramping up to heavily armored citadel units
Valakkar boss attacks: irradiated bile spit (ranged) and a ground-slam (AoE) that can quickly down unprepared players
Facility turrets and reactive AI guards at the ASD data centers
tips: Go as a team. The card-print defense, powering/operating the ATLS IKTI, and the boss DPS check all strongly favor a coordinated group.
Bring a radiation suit (Stirling exploration suit or equivalent) before heading to Pyro I, plus spare meds; the rad zones will kill an unprotected player fast.
During the boss fight, ignore the body and concentrate fire on the glowing green pustules - that is where the damage lands.
Watch for and side-step the bile spit and ground slam rather than tanking them.
Treat other players as a live threat the whole time; stage your extraction and don't linger at the freight elevators with full loot.
Carry the maintenance key card forward so you can power the ATLS IKTI without backtracking.
